# Fundamentals: Units, Conventions, Lowering

## Units

The kernel (`Protocol`, Hamiltonian IR, backends) works in **seconds** and
**rad/s** throughout: protocol parameters, drive coefficients, and
interaction strengths are all angular frequencies in rad/s, times in
seconds.

## Level structures: names carry semantics

Preset *names* encode Hamiltonian construction; `param_set` only switches
numerical parameter sets within identical semantics:

- `1r` / `01r` — symbolic two-/three-level lattices, blocks driven by
  protocols;
- `analog_3` — *physical* Rb87 analog three-level blocks (static `H_1013`,
  decay-capable `H_const`);
- `rb87_7` — full seven-level Rb87 gate model (`param_set="our"` or
  `"lukin"`).

Fully custom symbolic models are hand-built `LevelStructureSpec` instances
passed straight to `RydbergSystem.from_lattice` — they never mount physical
blocks, regardless of `param_set` (Decision Log D11/D13).

## One kernel, one control surface

There is exactly **one** simulation system. A continuous-time `Protocol` —
functions of time plus an optimization parameter vector `x`, resolved
against the physical system at compile time — is bound to a
`RydbergSystem`, lowered to the unified Hamiltonian IR, and evolved by the
selected backend:

```
TOProtocol / SweepProtocol ────┐
TFIMQuenchProtocol / ...       ├─► RydbergSystem (+ bound Protocol)
DigitalAnalogProtocol ─────────┘        └─ compile_hamiltonian_ir
                                             └─ backend (exact / mps / peps)
                                                  └─ EvolutionResult
```

Protocols emit drive coefficients per compiler channel
(`get_drive_coefficients(t, params)`); per-site addressing uses compiler
keys `f"{base}_{site}"`, resolved by the kernel channel-lowering rules
(`split_site_channel` / `block_name_for_drive_channel` in
`core.level_structures`).

## Geometry conventions

Chain sublattice signs are `(-1)**i`, rectangle `(-1)**(row+col)`,
triangular all-zero; `Register.from_coordinates` infers spacing from the
smallest positive sorted-x difference.

## Serialization

Reproducibility objects (`Register`, `RegisterLayout`,
`LevelStructureSpec`, `NoiseModel`, `CZGateReport`) write a plain dict
tagged `"schema": "ryd-gate/<kind>/v1"` and reconstruct via `from_dict`.
The `v1` payloads are frozen as JSON Schema files in `ryd_gate/schemas/`;
install the `schema` extra for optional validation via
`validate_json_schema`.
